name: How can I make Cortana understand my voice better so voice search stops mishearing everything?

mainEntity.name: How can I make Cortana understand my voice better so voice search stops mishearing everything?

acceptedAnswer.text:
To make Cortana understand you better, you need to retrain speech recognition, set the right language and region, and use a good microphone.

Cortana relies on Windows’ underlying speech recognition and your profile to interpret your voice correctly.

**Key factors**
- Match speech language to your accent and region in Time & Language settings
- Run Windows Speech Recognition setup and training to build a profile
- Use a headset or directional mic to cut room noise
- Avoid talking over others or far away from the device

In noisy apartments in Los Angeles or busy households in Austin, physical setup matters as much as software—place the mic close and reduce background audio when using voice search.

Think of Cortana accuracy as a mix of tuning the system and shaping the environment where you speak.
acceptedAnswer.description: Improve Cortana’s accuracy by matching your speech language and region, running Windows Speech Recognition training to create a tailored voice profile, and using a quality microphone in a quiet environment. These changes dramatically reduce misheard commands and wrong voice‑search results.
